module.name: common
module.purpose: Shared common crate root that wires reusable config loading (now including the ToolConfig tool directory setting re-exported from lib.rs), now-public crate-local external re-export adapters, logging, protocol models (whose SessionMessage between-session envelope and the step protocol types StepKind/ModelStepKind/ExecutionStepKind/UserStepKind/StepSignature/StepEvent/StepResult/StepStatus/StepPreview are now re-exported from lib.rs alongside SessionEvent), and structure utilities; the tool schema/preview contracts now live in the protocol module and the Tool executable handle now lives in the host crate's executor module (host/executor/tool.rs), so common no longer owns a tool module. Concrete selectable interfaces live in child modules while lib.rs remains namespace and re-export wiring.

childModules.name: config
childModules.purpose: Owns the common crate configuration model, config-file discovery, platform detection, alias substitution, credential resolution, runtime path normalization, and the new ToolConfig tool directory setting whose path load_config resolves relative to the config root.

childModules.name: external
childModules.purpose: Owns now-public re-export wrappers for third-party libraries so downstream crates route third-party access through common; single ownership stays in common.

childModules.name: logging
childModules.purpose: Owns tagged log messages, logging errors, config-gated log writes, process-role path selection, log-file creation, and pruning.

childModules.name: protocol
childModules.purpose: Owns shared session, task, and tool-execution protocol interface types: identifiers, signatures, session events, statuses, updates, the SessionMessage between-session network envelope that wraps a SessionEvent with a timestamp and source_name, and the ToolSchema/ToolPreview tool metadata contracts relocated from the removed tool module. The step protocol contracts (StepKind, StepSignature, StepEvent, StepResult, StepStatus, StepPreview) now live in the step child module, and the execution protocol contracts (ExecutionEvent, ExecutionRequest, ExecutionUpdate, ExecutionSignature, ExecutionStatus) now live in the execution child module.

childModules.name: structure
childModules.purpose: Owns shared structural data interfaces: the generic WorkQueue (now offering a with(key, closure) borrowing accessor that runs a closure on a borrowed &V, checking working then complete, so callers can use a non-Clone value in place) and the channel helpers, whose remote NetSender/NetReceiver endpoints are now remoc rch::mpsc channels bootstrapped over a base connection handshake and shared behind std::sync::Mutex.
